result.summary: Printify exposes a REST API for merchants and platforms, authenticated either with a personal access token or OAuth 2.0 bearer tokens.

printify_personal_access_token.label: Printify personal access token
printify_personal_access_token.setup: Create a Printify account, then in the Printify app go to Connections from the flow described in the [Printify API docs](https://developers.printify.com/). Generate a Personal Access Token, choose its access scopes, and copy it immediately because it is shown only once. Send it as `Authorization: Bearer {token}`.

printify_oauth_app.label: Printify OAuth app credentials
printify_oauth_app.setup: Register your integration using the application form linked from the [Printify API docs](https://developers.printify.com/). If approved, Printify emails you instructions for receiving your app ID and setting required access scopes. You then use that `app_id` in the OAuth flow at [https://printify.com/](https://printify.com/) to obtain merchant tokens.

app_id.description: Application ID provided by Printify after app approval.

printify_oauth_access_token.label: Printify OAuth 2.0 access token
printify_oauth_access_token.setup: First register your app as described in the [Printify API docs](https://developers.printify.com/). Then send the merchant through the OAuth flow described there to obtain an access token and refresh token from the Printify OAuth endpoints. Use the access token as `Authorization: Bearer {token}`.

surfaces.name: Printify REST API

requiredHeaders.name: User-Agent
requiredHeaders.description: Required on all requests; docs say every request must specify a `User-Agent` header with the client type or application name.

variables.name: shop_id
variables.description: Shop identifier used in paths like `/v1/shops/{shop_id}/...`; retrieve it by listing shops first, as described in the docs.

surfaces.notes: The docs state that unless otherwise mentioned, all endpoints support both Personal Access Token and OAuth 2.0 authentication. Requests are JSON responses over HTTPS, with form-encoded request bodies in V1.
